# PongLens

> PongLens turns table tennis match videos into something you can study. Point your phone at the table, play your match, and upload the recording. PongLens cuts it down to just the rallies, splits the match into individual points, and maps where the ball lands.

PongLens is a match-analysis tool for table tennis players. You record a match, upload the video (or paste a YouTube link), and PongLens returns a cut containing only live play, one clip per point, placement maps, and a scorecard you can share. You can add notes to any point and share your matches with a coach, who can leave notes of their own.

## What it does today
- Removes dead time between points, turning a long recording into just the rallies.
- Cuts the match into individual point clips, with serve and winner detected.
- Placement maps: where serves, receives, and rally balls land on the table.
- Live scorecard you can share, plus exportable score reels.
- Notes on any point, including voice notes with transcripts.
- Coach sharing: a coach can view shared matches and leave notes.

## In development
- Progress tracking: notes across matches and lessons organized into what to work on next.

## Pages
- [Home](https://www.ponglens.com/): Product overview and what PongLens does.
- [Sign in](https://www.ponglens.com/login): Sign in to upload and analyze a match.
- [Terms of Service](https://www.ponglens.com/terms): Terms that govern use of PongLens.
- [Privacy Policy](https://www.ponglens.com/privacy): How PongLens handles your videos and data.
